title: agalinis linarioides subsp. rojasii
intro: Agalinis linarioides subsp. rojasii, also known as Rojas' agalinis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutritional needs by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The medicinal properties of Rojas' agalinis include its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Rojas' agalinis, such as infusions and decoctions, have been traditionally used to treat fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, as well as to support digestion and boost the immune system.
